abs.description: Abs returns the absolute value of x.

acos.description: Acos returns the arccosine, in radians, of x.

acosh.description: Acosh returns the inverse hyperbolic cosine of x.

Antivortex.description:
Antivortex magnetization with given circulation and core polarization

### Methods
`Add(float64, Config)`, `RotZ(float64)`, `Scale(float64, float64, float64)` and `Transl(float64, float64, float64)`.

asin.description: Asin returns the arcsine, in radians, of x.

asinh.description: Asinh returns the inverse hyperbolic sine of x.

atan.description: Atan returns the arctangent, in radians, of x.

atan2.description: Atan2 returns the arc tangent of y/x, using the signs of the two to     determine the quadrant of the return value.

atanh.description: Atanh returns the inverse hyperbolic tangent of x.

AutoSave.description: Auto save space-dependent quantity every period (s).

AutoSnapshot.description: Auto save image of quantity every period (s).

BlochSkyrmion.description:
Bloch skyrmion magnetization with given chirality and core polarization

### Methods
`Add(float64, Config)`, `RotZ(float64)`, `Scale(float64, float64, float64)` and `Transl(float64, float64, float64)`.

cbrt.description: Cbrt returns the cube root of x.

ceil.description: Ceil returns the least integer value greater than or equal to x.

Cell.description:
Single cell with given integer index (i, j, k)

### Methods
`Add(Shape)`, `Intersect(Shape)`, `Inverse()`, `Repeat(float64, float64, float64)`, `RotX(float64)`, `RotY(float64)`, `RotZ(float64)`, `Scale(float64, float64, float64)`, `Sub(Shape)`, `Transl(float64, float64, float64)` and `Xor(Shape)`.

CellIndices.description:
4D slice containing the index of each cell as (ix, iy, iz).

ClearPostSteps.description: Clear the postStep array, which contains functions that are executed after each solver step. This includes running averages, centering routines to track skyrmions and domain walls etc.

Cone.description:
3D Cone with diameter and height in meter. The base is at z=0.If the height is positive, the tip points in the +z direction.

### Methods

Conical state for given wave vector, cone direction, and cone angle

### Methods
`Add(float64, Config)`, `RotZ(float64)`, `Scale(float64, float64, float64)` and `Transl(float64, float64, float64)`.

cos.description: Cos returns the cosine of the radian argument x.

cosh.description: Cosh returns the hyperbolic cosine of x.

Crop.signature: Crop(Quantity, int, int, int, int, int, int) *cropped
Crop.description:
Crops a quantity to cell ranges [x1,x2[, [y1,y2[, [z1,z2[

### Methods
`Average()` and `EvalTo(Slice)`.

CropLayer.description:
Crops a quantity to a single layer

### Methods
`Average()` and `EvalTo(Slice)`.

CropRegion.description:
Crops a quantity to a region

### Methods
`Average()` and `EvalTo(Slice)`.

CropX.description:
Crops a quantity to cell ranges [x1,x2[

### Methods
`Average()` and `EvalTo(Slice)`.

CropY.description:
Crops a quantity to cell ranges [y1,y2[

### Methods
`Average()` and `EvalTo(Slice)`.

CropZ.description:
Crops a quantity to cell ranges [z1,z2[

### Methods

Cross product of two vector quantities

Cuboid.description:
Cuboid with sides in meter

`Add(Shape)`, `Intersect(Shape)`, `Inverse()`, `Repeat(float64, float64, float64)`, `RotX(float64)`, `RotY(float64)`, `RotZ(float64)`, `Scale(float64, float64, float64)`, `Sub(Shape)`, `Transl(float64, float64, float64)` and `Xor(Shape)`.

CurrentMag.description:
Returns the current magnetization as a Config. E.g. CurrentMag().Add(0.1, RandomMagSeed(123)) will return a Config with the current magnetization plus some noise.

### Methods
`Add(float64, Config)`, `RotZ(float64)`, `Scale(float64, float64, float64)` and `Transl(float64, float64, float64)`.

CustomQuantity.description:
Creates an arbitrary custom Quantity from a user provided (scalar or vector) slice. The slice should match the simulation grid size, and can be created with NewScalarMask or NewVectorMask, or loaded in via Loadfile. Variations in slice values are not restricted to the 256 region limit.

### Methods
`EvalTo()`.

3D Cylinder with diameter and height in meter

DefRegion.description: Define a material region with given index (0-255) and shape

DefRegionCell.description: Set a material region (first argument) in one cell by the index of the cell (last three arguments)

Div.description:
Point-wise division of two quantities

### Methods
`EvalTo()`.

Dot.description:
Dot product of two vector quantities

### Methods
`EvalTo()`.

Ellipse.description:
2D Ellipse with axes in meter

3D Ellipsoid with axes in meter

erf.description: Erf returns the error function of x.

erfc.description: Erfc returns the complementary error function of x.

exp.description: Exp returns e**x, the base-e exponential of x.

exp2.description: Exp2 returns 2**x, the base-2 exponential of x.

Expect.description: Used for automated tests: checks if a value is close enough to the expected value

ExpectB.description: Used for automated tests: checks if two booleans are equal

ExpectV.description: Used for automated tests: checks if a vector is close enough to the expected value

expm1.description: Expm1 returns e**x - 1, the base-e exponential of x minus 1. It is more     accurate than Exp(x) - 1 when x is near zero.

ext_AllRegionShapes.description: Returns a function that gives the shape of each region. Does not automatically update if the region is redefined or moved with Shift. Call as: allregions := ext_AllRegionShapes(); shape := allregions(i); on seperate lines. Note: double parentheses as ext_AllRegionShapes()(i) are not supported in mx3 scripts and must be split across two lines.

ext_centerBubble.description: centerBubble shifts m after each step to keep the bubble position close to the center of the window

ext_centerWall.description: centerWall(c) shifts m after each step to keep m_c close to zero

ext_centerWallInLayer.description: centerWallInLayer(L, c) shifts m after each step to keep m_c in layer L close to zero

ext_centerWallInRegion.description: centerWallInRegion(R, c) shifts m after each step to keep m_c in region R close to zero

ext_EnableUnsafe.description: Deprecated. Only here to ensure maximal backwards compatibility with mumax3.9c.

ext_GeomEdge.description:
Returns an edge (+x, -x, +y, -y, +z, -z) of the geometry as a Shape. The Shape may not update automatically after changes to the mesh or geometry; rerun the function to refresh.

### Methods
`Add(Shape)`, `Intersect(Shape)`, `Inverse()`, `Repeat(float64, float64, float64)`, `RotX(float64)`, `RotY(float64)`, `RotZ(float64)`, `Scale(float64, float64, float64)`, `Sub(Shape)`, `Transl(float64, float64, float64)` and `Xor(Shape)`.

ext_grainboundaries.description: (startregion, numgrains, offset, boundarythickness, zeroflag). Given existing regions, reassigns grain boundaries of boundarythickness to new region values, starting at offset. Zeroflag: 1 = region0 is normal, 0 = region0 acts as edge but no boundary itself, -1 = ignore region0 entirely. grainboundary_edgeX/Y/Z control whether simulatiion box edges are treated as grainboundaries.

ext_InitGeomFromOVF.description: Initialize geometry, cell count and cell size given a pattern from OVF

ext_InterDind.description: Sets Dind coupling between two regions.

ext_InterExchange.description: Sets exchange coupling between two regions.

ext_make3dgrains.description: 3D Voronoi tesselation over shape (grain size, starting region number, num regions, shape, seed)

ext_makegrains.description: Voronoi tesselation (grain size, num regions, seed)

ext_rmSurfaceCharge.description: Compensate magnetic charges on the left and right sides of an in-plane magnetized wire. Arguments: region, mx on left and right side, resp.

ext_ScaleDind.description: Re-scales Dind coupling between two regions.

ext_ScaleExchange.description: Re-scales exchange coupling between two regions.

floor.description: Floor returns the greatest integer value less than or equal to x.

Flush.description: Flush all pending output to disk.

FunctionFromDatafile.signature: FunctionFromDatafile(string, int, int, string) func(float64), float64
FunctionFromDatafile.description: Creates an interpolation function using data from two columns in a csv file. Arguments: filename, xColumnIdx, yColumnIdx, method ("linear", "nearest" or "step").

gamma.description: Gamma returns the Gamma function of x.

GrainRoughness.description:
Grainy surface with different heights per grain with a typical grain size (first argument), minimal height (second argument), and maximal height (third argument). The last argument is a seed for the random number generator.

### Methods
`Add(Shape)`, `Intersect(Shape)`, `Inverse()`, `Repeat(float64, float64, float64)`, `RotX(float64)`, `RotY(float64)`, `RotZ(float64)`, `Scale(float64, float64, float64)`, `Sub(Shape)`, `Transl(float64, float64, float64)` and `Xor(Shape)`.

heaviside.description: Returns 1 if x>0, 0 if x<0, and 0.5 if x==0

Helical.description:
Helical state for given wave vector

### Methods
`Add(float64, Config)`, `RotZ(float64)`, `Scale(float64, float64, float64)` and `Transl(float64, float64, float64)`.

HopfionCompactSupport.description:
Hopfion texture from skyrmion, with compact support (smooth and magnetization exactly along z-axis outside of finite region)

### Methods
`Add(float64, Config)`, `RotZ(float64)`, `Scale(float64, float64, float64)` and `Transl(float64, float64, float64)`.

hypot.description: Hypot returns Sqrt(p*p + q*q), taking care to avoid unnecessary overflow and     underflow.

ilogb.description: Ilogb returns the binary exponent of x as an integer.

ImageShape.description:
Use black/white image as shape

### Methods
`Add(Shape)`, `Intersect(Shape)`, `Inverse()`, `Repeat(float64, float64, float64)`, `RotX(float64)`, `RotY(float64)`, `RotZ(float64)`, `Scale(float64, float64, float64)`, `Sub(Shape)`, `Transl(float64, float64, float64)` and `Xor(Shape)`.

Index2Coord.description:
Convert cell index to x,y,z coordinate in meter

isInf.description: IsInf reports whether f is an infinity, according to sign. If sign > 0,     IsInf reports whether f is positive infinity. If sign < 0, IsInf reports     whether f is negative infinity. If sign == 0, IsInf reports whether f is     either infinity.

isNaN.description: IsNaN reports whether f is an IEEE 754 “not-a-number” value.

j0.description: J0 returns the order-zero Bessel function of the first kind.

j1.description: J1 returns the order-one Bessel function of the first kind.

jn.description: Jn returns the order-n Bessel function of the first kind.

Layer.description:
Single layer (along z), by integer index starting from 0. Note: based on the current cell size, so may no longer be valid if cell size is changed.

### Methods
`Add(Shape)`, `Intersect(Shape)`, `Inverse()`, `Repeat(float64, float64, float64)`, `RotX(float64)`, `RotY(float64)`, `RotZ(float64)`, `Scale(float64, float64, float64)`, `Sub(Shape)`, `Transl(float64, float64, float64)` and `Xor(Shape)`.

Layers.description:
Part of space between cell layer1 (inclusive) and layer2 (exclusive), in integer indices. Note: based on the current cell size, so may no longer be valid if cell size is changed.

### Methods
`Add(Shape)`, `Intersect(Shape)`, `Inverse()`, `Repeat(float64, float64, float64)`, `RotX(float64)`, `RotY(float64)`, `RotZ(float64)`, `Scale(float64, float64, float64)`, `Sub(Shape)`, `Transl(float64, float64, float64)` and `Xor(Shape)`.

ldexp.description: Ldexp is the inverse of Frexp. It returns frac × 2**exp.

Line.signature: Line(float64, float64, float64, float64, float64, float64, float64, string) Shape
Line.description:
3D line segment between (x1, y1, z1) and (x2, y2, z2), with given diameter, in meter.Last element specifies the line cap, which can be 'infinite', 'round' or 'flat'.Using zero diameter creates a minimally connected geometry, unless it is later scaled/rotated.

### Methods
`Add(Shape)`, `Intersect(Shape)`, `Inverse()`, `Repeat(float64, float64, float64)`, `RotX(float64)`, `RotY(float64)`, `RotZ(float64)`, `Scale(float64, float64, float64)`, `Sub(Shape)`, `Transl(float64, float64, float64)` and `Xor(Shape)`.

Line2D.signature: Line2D(float64, float64, float64, float64, float64, string) Shape
Line2D.description:
2D equivalent of Line(), resulting in a uniform fill along the z-axis

### Methods
`Add(Shape)`, `Intersect(Shape)`, `Inverse()`, `Repeat(float64, float64, float64)`, `RotX(float64)`, `RotY(float64)`, `RotZ(float64)`, `Scale(float64, float64, float64)`, `Sub(Shape)`, `Transl(float64, float64, float64)` and `Xor(Shape)`.

Load a data file (ovf or dump)

log.description: Log returns the natural logarithm of x.

log10.description: Log10 returns the decimal logarithm of x. The special cases are the same as     for Log.

log1p.description: Log1p returns the natural logarithm of 1 plus its argument x. It is more     accurate than Log(1 + x) when x is near zero.

log2.description: Log2 returns the binary logarithm of x. The special cases are the same as     for Log.

logb.description: Logb returns the binary exponent of x.

Madd.description:
Weighted addition: Madd(Q1,Q2,c1,c2) = c1*Q1 + c2*Q2

### Methods
`EvalTo(Slice)`.

max.description: Max returns the larger of x or y.

min.description: Min returns the smaller of x or y.

Minimize.description: Use steepest conjugate gradient method to minimize the total energy. Returns true if convergence is reached, or false if the wall-clock time limit is exceeded. The wall-clock time limit is disabled by default.

mod.description: Mod returns the floating-point remainder of x/y. The magnitude of the result     is less than y and its sign agrees with that of x.

Mul.description:
Point-wise product of two quantities

### Methods
`EvalTo()`.

MulMV.description:
Matrix-Vector product: MulMV(AX, AY, AZ, m) = (AX·m, AY·m, AZ·m). The arguments Ax, Ay, Az and m are quantities with 3 componets.

### Methods
`EvalTo()`.

pow.description: Pow returns x**y, the base-x exponential of y.

pow10.description: Pow10 returns 10**n, the base-10 exponential of n.

randExp.description: Exponentially distributed random number between 0 and +inf, mean=1

Random magnetization

RedefRegion.description: Reassign all cells with a given region (first argument) to a new region (second argument)

Relax.description: Try to minimize the total energy. Returns true if convergence is reached, or false if the wall-clock time limit is exceeded. The wall-clock time limit is disabled by default.

remainder.description: Remainder returns the IEEE 754 floating-point remainder of x/y.

Run.description: Run the simulation for a time in seconds

RunningAverage.description:
Records the time-average of a quantity from the moment this function is called.Note: this may impact performance since the Quantity will be evaluated after every step.

### Methods
`EvalTo()`.

RunWhile.description: Run while condition function is true

Save.description: Save space-dependent quantity once, with auto filename

SaveAs.description: Save space-dependent quantity with custom filename

SetCellSize.description: Sets the X,Y,Z cell size in meters

SetGeom.description: Sets the geometry to a given shape

SetGridSize.description: Sets the number of cells for X,Y,Z

SetMesh.signature: SetMesh(int, int, int, float64, float64, float64, int, int, int)
SetMesh.description: Sets GridSize, CellSize and PBC at the same time

SetPBC.description: Sets the number of repetitions in X,Y,Z to create periodic boundary conditions. The number of repetitions determines the cutoff range for the demagnetization.

SetSolver.description: Set solver type.1: Euler2: Heun3: Bogacki-Shampine4: Runge-Kutta (RK4)5: Dormand-Prince6: Fehlberg-1: Backward Euler

Shift.description: Shifts the simulation by +1/-1 cells along X

sin.description: Sin returns the sine of the radian argument x.

sinc.description: Sinc returns sin(x)/x. If x=0, then Sinc(x) returns 1.

since.description:
Returns the time elapsed since argument

### Methods
`Abs()`, `Hours()`, `Microseconds()`, `Milliseconds()`, `Minutes()`, `Nanoseconds()`, `Round(time.Duration)`, `Seconds()` and `Truncate(time.Duration)`.

sinh.description: Sinh returns the hyperbolic sine of x.

SnapshotAs.description: Save image of quantity with custom filename

sprint.description: Print all arguments to string with automatic formatting

sprintf.description: Print to string with C-style formatting.

sqrt.description: Sqrt returns the square root of x.

Square.description:
2D square with size in meter

Steps.description: Run the simulation for a number of time steps

Sum.description: Sum of Quantity over all cells in the grid. For a vector Quantity, all components are added together.

SumVector.description:
Sum of vector Quantity over all cells in the grid.

Superball.description:
3D Superball with diameter in meter and shape parameter p.Interpolates between a cube (p=+∞), sphere (p=1), octahedron (p=0.5) and empty space (p≤0).

TableSave.description: Save the data table right now (appends one line).

tan.description: Tan returns the tangent of the radian argument x.

tanh.description: Tanh returns the hyperbolic tangent of x.

ThermSeed.description: Set a random seed for thermal noise

xi.description:
Non-adiabaticity of spin-transfer-torque

### Methods
`Average()`, `EvalTo(Slice)`, `GetRegion(int)`, `IsUniform()`, `MSlice()`, `Region(int)`, `Set(float64)`, `SetRegion(int, ScalarFunction)`, `SetRegionFuncGo(int, func() float64)` and `SetRegionValueGo(int, float64)`.

EvalTo.description: Evaluates this quantity and returns a new slice containing the result.

Evaluates this quantity into the provided slice object.
A slice can, for instance, be created with `NewScalarMask`, `NewVectorMask` or `LoadFile`.

Add.description: Adds a scaled configuration to the current configuration.
Add this shape to the original shape.
Add an extra mask*multiplier term to the excitation.

RotZ.description: Rotate the shape about the Z-axis by a given angle in radians.

Scale.description: Scales the magnetization or configuration by the provided factors.

Transl.description: Translates a vector configuration in x, y, and z directions.

Intersect.description: Intersection between this shape and the original shape.

Repeat.description: Repeat the shape every (dx, dy, dz) in space.

RotX.description: Rotate the shape about the X-axis by a given angle in radians.

RotY.description: Rotate the shape about the Y-axis by a given angle in radians.

Sub.description: Subtract this shape from the original shape.

Xor.description: Exclusive OR with the original shape.

CPUAccess.description:
Returns whether the Slice is accessible by the CPU.
 `true` means it is stored in host memory.

Comp.description: Accesses a component of a vector quantity.

DevPtr.description: Returns a CUDA device pointer to a component. Slice must have GPUAccess. It is safe to call on a nil slice, returns NULL.

Disable.description: INTERNAL. Overwrite struct fields with zeros to avoid accidental use after Free.

Free.description: Frees the GPU memory and disables the slice.

GPUAccess.description:
Returns whether the Slice is accessible by the GPU.
 `true` means it is either stored on GPU or in unified host memory.

Get.description: Get the value at (comp, ix, iy, iz) of this slice.
Returns the average value, either as `float64` or `data.Vector`.

Host.description: Returns the slice as type `[][]float32` indexed by component and cell number. Requires `CPUAccess() == true`.

HostCopy.description: Returns a copy of the Slice, allocated on CPU.

Index.description: Returns the flat index of (ix, iy, iz).

IsNil.description: Returns `true` if either this slice or its pointer are `nil`.

Len.description:
If called on a
- Slice: returns the number of elements per component
- Vector: returns its norm.

MemType.description: Returns 1 (2) if this slice resides on the CPU (GPU).

Scalars.description: Returns the data as 3D array (type `[][][]float32`), indexed by cell position. Data should be scalar (1 component) and have `CPUAccess() == true`.

Set.description: Set the value at (comp, ix, iy, iz) of this slice to a given scalar value.
Sets this quantity to a uniform scalar value in all regions.
Set this excitation to a constant vector.
Set the magnetization to the given configuration.

SetScalar.description: Set the value at index (ix, iy, iz) of a scalar slice.

SetVector.description: Set the value at index (ix, iy, iz) of a vector slice.

Size.description: Returns the size of this slice as a `[3]int`.

Tensors.description: Returns the data as 4D array (type `[][][][]float32`), indexed by component and cell position. Requires `CPUAccess() == true`.

Vectors.description: Returns the data as 4D array (type `[3][][][]float32`), indexed by component and cell position. Data should have 3 components and have `CPUAccess() == true`.

Average.description: Returns the spatial average of this quantity across the entire mesh.

Div.description: Divide the vector by a given amount.

MAdd.description: Multiply by float64 and add a Vector to the result.

Mul.description: Multiply this vector by a scalar value.

X.description: Get the X-component of this vector.

Y.description: Get the Y-component of this vector.

Z.description: Get the Z-component of this vector.

GetRegion.description: Returns the value of this quantity within the specified region.

IsUniform.description: Returns true if the field is uniform across all cells.

MSlice.description: Get the underlying `cuda.MSlice` of this quantity.

Region.description: Access the value of this quantity in the given region.

SetRegion.description: Set the value of this quantity in the given region to a scalar function.
Set the value of this quantity in the given region to a vector function.
Set the magnetization to the given configuration inside the specified region.

SetRegionFuncGo.description: Set the value of this quantity in the given region to a scalar function. Should only be used in a Go script.

SetRegionValueGo.description: Set the value of this quantity in the given region to a fixed scalar value. Should only be used in a Go script.

SetRegionFn.description: Set the value of this excitation in a given region. Should only be used in a Go script.

AddGo.description: An `Add(mask, f)` equivalent for Go use.

AddTo.description: Add the values of this field to the provided slice.

RemoveExtraTerms.description: After resizing the mesh, the extra terms don't fit the grid anymore and there is no reasonable way to resize them. So remove them and have the user re-add them.

GetCell.description: Get the value at index (ix, iy, iz).

Gpu.description: Return a slice that lives on the GPU.

Buffer.description: Return a slice that lives on the GPU.

LoadFile.description: Load from a previously saved file.

Quantity.description: Return the average magnetization as type `[]float64`.

SetArray.description: Set the magnetization based on the provided slice.

SetCell.description: Set the magnetization at index (ix, iy, iz) to a given vector.
Set the region at index (ix, iy, iz) to a particular value (last argument).

SetInShape.description: Set the magnetization to a given configuration, but only inside the provided shape.

HostArray.description: Returns the regions throughout the mesh as type `[][][]byte`.

HostList.description: Returns the regions throughout the flattened mesh as type `[]byte`.
